channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Edwards leaves FSG and Liverpool ahead of new season
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Michael Edwards has left his role as chief executive officer (CEO) of football for Fenway Sports Group (FSG), who own Liverpool, leaving the club ahead of the 2026/27 season.

item.title: 'That's a way to make a 3!' | Scott holes out from fairway at Scottish Open
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Adam Scott holes out from 178 yards for birdie on the second hole at the Genesis Scottish Open.

item.title: Police nab suspect over alleged theft of N3bn from financial institution
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The PSFU spokesperson said that the suspect was apprehended following an extensive investigation into a sophisticated cyber intrusion targeting a financial institution.

item.title: Florida ransomware negotiator convicted for helping ransomware gang extort US companies
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: A third ransomware negotiator has been jailed for helping a notorious ransomware group to extort American victim companies into paying the hackers.

item.title: Police restrict movement ahead of Edo local government elections
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: As part of efforts to ensure peaceful local government elections in the state, the Edo State Police Command has announced a restriction of movement from 12 midnight on Friday to 3 pm on Saturday. A statement issued on Friday by the command’s Police Public Relations Off

item.title: How Nigerians can submit fraud petitions online — EFCC
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has explained how Nigerians can submit petitions on alleged fraud and other financial crimes through its online portal. To submit a petition, applicants are required to complete an online form with their personal information. The required details i

item.title: Gen Z-ers share how they’re achieving financial freedom — and the old ideas they’re leaving behind
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: A stable job and a nice home have become less accessible to young Americans. Many are taking new paths to pursue their dreams.

item.title: Tinubu Commissions Wasa Informal Sector Layout, Says Infrastructure Must Drive Economic Inclusion
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Tinubu on Friday commissioned the access roads, power and water supply to the Informal Sector Layout in Wasa District, Abuja, declaring that infrastructure development under his administration is designed to promote economic inclusion, empower small businesses and improve the liveliho

item.title: NDLEA Arraigns Drug Baron Anochili, 3 Mexicans, 6 Others, Remanded In Prison
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: A 63-year-old drug baron, Anochili Innocent, three Mexican nationals and six other Nigerians have been arraigned before Justice Musa Kakaki of the Federal High Court, Lagos, on an 11-count criminal charge bordering on the setting up of a super clandestine laboratory and the production of 2,419.48 ki

item.title: This Nigerian microfinance bank’s slow-lending strategy is delivering fast results
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: Speed is a competitive advantage in Nigeria's $2.1 billion digital lending market. Fintech lenders promise approvals in minutes, using automation to move borrowers from application to disbursement with as little friction as possible.

item.title: PDP Raises Concern Over Alia’s Weak Responses To Herdsmen Killings In Benue
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …Says state mustn’t be turned into a slaughter ground The People’s Democratic Party (PDP) in Benue State, on Thursday, expressed profound concern at the weak responses of the state government under the leadership of Governor Hyacinth Alia over incessant killing of citizens by inva

item.title: Wave5Wireless CEO Explains Why Nigeria’s 5G Isn’t Taking Off as Expected
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Coverage at 14%, usage at just 5% – four years after commercial launch, industry voices say Nigeria is “planting flags” instead of building a network | By: Francis Onyemachi Nigeria has set a target of 30 per cent 5G network penetrations under its National Broadband Plan, but

item.title: Qing Madi becomes new face of Infinix HOT 70 Pro, set for UNILAG Campus Storm
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Infinix has officially announced Nigerian music sensation Qing Madias the face of its all-new HOT 70 Pro, marking the beginning of an exciting campaign that celebrates creativity, individuality and fearless self-expression.​ The partnership brings together one of Africa’s fastest-rising music

item.title: Troops foil multiple infiltration attempts in Borno, force terrorists into retreat
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of the Joint Task Force North East, Operation HADIN KAI, OPHK, foiled multiple coordinated infiltration attempts by ISWAP terrorists across Borno State in the early hours of Friday. A statement signed by Captain Mohammed Goni, the Acting Military Information Officer, Headquarters, Joint

item.title: From Rotten Fruit To Renewable Power: Inside The Lagos Market Turning Waste Into Wealth
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: From lowering operational costs for food vendors and phone chargers to illuminating stalls and curbing local crime, this circular economy blueprint demonstrates how urban waste can be successfully repurposed to power small businesses and combat climate change. The post From Rotten Fruit To Renewable

item.title: What affordability crisis? Rich home buyers are powering the housing market.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “The housing market is the best expression of the ‘K-shaped economy’ that we’re in right now,” one expert says.

item.title: Ex-Arsenal Star Questioned By Police Over Money Laundering Involving Organised Drug Traffickers
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Samir Nasri was reportedly taken into custody in connection with a money laundering case involving alleged organised drug traffickers. French publication Le Parisien (via talkSPORT) claims that the former Arsenal and Manchester City star spent ten hours in custody before being released on Thursday.

item.title: Nnamdi Kanu’s Wife Denies Filing For Divorce, Dismisses Reports As False
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The wife of the detained leader of the Indigenous People of Biafra (IPOB), Nnamdi Kanu, Uchechi Okwu-Kanu, has dismissed reports claiming she filed for divorce, describing the allegations as false and misleading. In a statement posted on her verified X handle on Friday, Okwu-Kanu said her attention

item.title: Lawmaker backs legislative bill on femicide
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: It added that the chairperson agreed to review the draft bill with her legal team and rework it to meet legislative standards before its submission to the National Assembly for consideration. The post Lawmaker backs legislative bill on femicide appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.description: * Seeks lawyers, CSOs, others protection of justice Alex Enumah in Abuja  The Chief Justice of Nigeria (CJN), Justice Kudirat Kekere-Ekun, has charged judicial officers to do everything within their

item.title: Residents count losses as torrential rain floods Lagos communities
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Some Lagos residents are counting heavy losses after torrential rain flooded homes, destroyed businesses, trapped families and disrupted movement across several communities in the state. The post Residents count losses as torrential rain floods Lagos communities appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Ukrainian court detains alleged killers of Monaco bomb attack suspect
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Motives for the killing of Anastasiia Berezovska remain unclear as the unfolding saga grips both countries.

item.title: La dette cachée freine le développement du Sénégal: Next Africa
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Les grands projets d’infrastructure devaient incarner la transformation du Sénégal en l’une des économies les plus dynamiques d’Afrique de l’Ouest.

item.title: Ogun: Man, 47, docked over N870,000 bank impersonation fraud
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Laide Raheem, Abeokuta A 47-year-old man, Idris Balogun, has been arraigned before Abeokuta Magistrates’ Court in Isabo, over an alleged N870,000 fraud by impersonating as a bank employee. Balogun is facing a six-count charge bordering on impersonation, obtaining by false pretence and stealing.

item.title: I’m filling in at The Verge for 6 weeks. Ask me anything!

item.description: Hello my friends! My protocol pals! My computational compadres! I'm David, a technology reporter and co-host of the Waveform Podcast. item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …As supporters stage ‘victory walk’ in Ode-Omu The governorship candidate of the All Progressives Congress (APC) in the August 15, 2026 Osun State election, Asiwaju Munirudeen Bola Oyebamiji (AMBO), has urged residents not to be intimidated by threats or violence ahead of the poll.

item.title: Scheffler set to miss cut at Scottish Open
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Scottie Scheffler is set to miss the cut at the Genesis Scottish Open, ending a 78-tournament streak where the world No 1 has made it through to the weekend.

item.title: Scheffler misses short putt as record cut streak set to end!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Scottie Scheffler misses a six-foot par putt on his final hole of the second round of the Genesis Scottish Open, as his streak of 78 cuts made is set to come to an end.

item.description: “By claiming earlier, I could preserve more of my portfolio and allow those assets to continue compounding.”

item.title: SK Hynix Indicated to Climb 17% After $26.5 Billion ADR Offering
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: SK Hynix Inc.’s American depositary receipts are indicated to open 17% above their offering price, after the South Korean memory chipmaker raised $26.5 billion in the largest-ever US listing by a foreign company.

item.description: Heading for Taiwan and south-eastern China, the 1,000 km-wide Bavi is forecast to be one of the strongest storms in decades.

item.dc:creator: Completesports

item.description: Though Instagram head Adam Mosseri doesn't want to filter out AI content on the platform, he argues that you "shouldn't have it in your feed" if you don't like it. item.title: NLS flays NBA’s objection, insists on conferment of ‘Blue Silk’ rank on lawyers
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Nigerian Law Society (NLS), an independent body of lawyers, has criticised the objection raised by the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) against its conferment of the Blue Silk rank on deserving legal practitioners in the country. The post NLS flays NBA’s objection, insists on conferment of &

item.title: FCCPC Wins Again As Court Upholds Probe Of Air Peace Fares
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has reaffirmed the powers of the Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission (FCCPC) to investigate consumer complaints relating to airline ticket pricing, handing the Commission another legal victory in its dispute with Air Peace Limited. In a judgment del

item.title: Ex-Super Eagles star fires back at Chelsea great Mikel over NFF criticism: “He’s very loud”
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Former Super Eagles winger and ex-Shooting Stars General Manager Dimeji Lawal has slammed John Obi Mikel over his recent attacks on the Nigeria Football Federation (NFF), Soccernet.ng reports. Lawal says the Chelsea legend should focus on providing practical solutions instead of repeatedly condemnin

item.title: Black Panther 3 Rumoured to Introduce Major Mutant to the MCU
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Black Panther 3 could play a key role in expanding the Marvel Cinematic Universe, with a new report claiming the highly anticipated sequel may introduce a major mutant character. According to entertainment insider James Mack, the next ‘Black Panther’ film is expected to feature a new mutant, potenti

item.title: MSMEs turn to innovation, tech to power growth amid challenges
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Nigeria’s small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) are defying the odds, leveraging innovation, technology, and a keen perception of opportunities to read more MSMEs turn to innovation, tech to power growth amid challenges

item.title: WAFCON 2026: CAF names Nigeria’s Akintoye among referees
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Confederation of African Football, CAF, has named Nigerian referee Yemisi Akintoye among the match officials for the upcoming 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations, DAILY POST reports. Akintoye became a FIFA-badged referee in 2021. The arbiter earned the appointment after successfully complet
